_doc: Chipyard resource-attribute injection rules. Categories selected via inject_resource_attrs.py --only <list>. Module keys are fnmatch patterns; a file matches if its stem OR any module declared inside it matches.

categories._doc_dsp: Move every multiply candidate toward DSP48. auto_dsp_module puts (* use_dsp48 = yes *) on any module whose body contains a multiply op; auto_dsp adds the same attribute to any assign/wire = ... * ...; expression. Explicit 'modules' entries pin specific Saturn multipliers (mul8 is opted out because DSP is 27x18 and 8x8 wastes a slice).
categories._doc_bram: Steer cache arrays to BRAM (overrides Vivado's LUTRAM choice on icache_tag); auto_ram classifies every other reg array by size+read-pattern: async-read -> distributed, sync-read >=2048b -> block, else auto.
categories._doc_cdc: CDC synchronizer hardening. ASYNC_REG packs the d3 chain into one slice for MTBF, shreg_extract=no prevents SRL packing that would break it. Off by default — turn on via --only cdc.
categories._doc_blanket: Uniform sledgehammer: stamp (* use_dsp48 = yes *) and (* ram_style = block *) on EVERY module decl in EVERY file. Vivado ignores the attribute on modules without applicable structures and falls back gracefully when block is incompatible. Use INJECT=blanket alone or stack INJECT=dsp,bram,blanket for max coverage (explicit per-signal opt-outs in dsp still win at signal scope).
